title: geography — trait composition
description: One owned trait: HasWorldLocalizedName for models that have a `name` + `iso2` column and a corresponding translation key at `world::country.<ISO2>`. Applied to Country today; the vendor does not ship translations for the other five entities so applying the trait there would be a no-op.

owned.description: Adds `resolveLocalizedName(?string $locale = null): string` + `getLocalizedNameAttribute(): string` (so `$country->localized_name` works). Resolves against `world::country.<ISO2>` translation catalog with English DB fallback via `Lang::has()` guard.

vendor.reason: Our Country model extends this. Same for the other five entities.

foundation.reason: Query builder integration for standard filter/sort/include envelope.

country.notes: Vendor uses integer PK + `$timestamps = false`. We do not override either. NO BelongsToTenant (reference-catalog data is global). NO SoftDeletes (vendor seeder truncates on world:install --force).

state.notes: No HasWorldLocalizedName — vendor ships no state translations.

language.notes: ISO-639 catalog. Referenced (NOT duplicated) by `localization::PlatformLanguage` via `platform_languages.geography_language_id` since Wave 5 — see module.json.naming_note + module.json.referenced_by.
